name: Use of eval()
description: eval() executes arbitrary Python code — a direct code injection risk.
fixHint: Use ast.literal_eval() for safe evaluation of literals.

name: Use of exec()
description: exec() executes arbitrary Python code dynamically.
fixHint: Avoid dynamic code execution. Restructure to use static function calls.

name: Use of pickle.loads() or pickle.load()
description: Deserializing pickle data from untrusted sources allows arbitrary code execution.
fixHint: Use JSON or another safe serialization format. Never unpickle data from untrusted sources.

name: subprocess with shell=True
description: shell=True passes the command to the shell interpreter, enabling command injection.
fixHint: Pass a list of arguments to subprocess functions and remove shell=True.

name: Raw SQL with % string formatting
description: Building SQL queries with % formatting allows SQL injection.
fixHint: Use parameterised queries: cursor.execute(sql, params) with a tuple of values.

name: yaml.load() without SafeLoader
description: yaml.load() without an explicit Loader can deserialize and execute arbitrary Python objects.
fixHint: Use yaml.safe_load() or pass Loader=yaml.SafeLoader explicitly.
